original_image_tags: A rectangular ink stamp at the bottom right reads "FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION / U. S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE". Below this, "AUG 7 1947" is stamped. Below the date, "SAN FRANCISCO" is typed, followed by "ROUTED TO" and a typed line ending in "FILE".

elements.description: A rectangular stamp or label located at the bottom right of the page, containing the text 'FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION', 'U. S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE', 'AUG 7 1947', and partially visible text 'SAN FRANCISCO' and 'ROUTED TO'.

The page consists of a typed memorandum from Headquarters Fourth Air Force, dated 4 August 1947, regarding the investigation of 'Flying Discs'. It is addressed to the FBI in San Francisco and signed by Major William R. Graham. The word 'CONFIDENTIAL' is stamped at the top and bottom of the page.

judge.reason: Both agree on a single visual element (the FBI stamp) with consistent core text and date, but B adds detail about the 'CONFIDENTIAL' stamp and typed memo content, while A adds the typed 'FILE' line, none of which contradict each other.
